def move_one_ball(arr):
    """This function checks whether an array of N unique integers can be sorted into non-decreasing order by applying right shift operations any number of times. A right shift moves every element one position to the right, with the last element wrapping around to the first position. If such sorting is possible, return True; otherwise, return False. An empty array is considered sorted, so return True for that case.

For example:
move_one_ball([3, 4, 5, 1, 2])==>True
This is because two right shifts result in a sorted array.
move_one_ball([3, 5, 4, 1, 2])==>False
No sequence of right shifts can sort this array.
